rule: for each result, paper_text = (title + ' ' + snippet).lower(); overlap = sum(1 for w in content_words if w.lower() in paper_text); hit forced=true if overlap >= 2; if overlap < 2, fresh agent decides hit based on whether the paper is functionally equivalent prior art at the same level of abstraction.

results.title: Reducing Cost of LLM Agents with Trajectory Reduction
results.reason: Only 'trajectory' substring matches; paper is about token-cost reduction of agent traces, no ergodic-theory mechanism or framing.

results.title: LLM-Agents-Papers: A repo lists papers related to LLM based agent
results.reason: Aggregator repo with zero content-word overlap and no specific prior-art claim.

results.title: Improving the Efficiency of LLM Agent Systems through Trajectory Reduction
results.reason: Same trajectory-cost-reduction work; addresses efficiency of trajectories, not their ergodic structure.

results.title: LEGOMem: Modular Procedural Memory for Multi-agent LLM
results.reason: Procedural-memory framework, zero content-word overlap, no ergodic content.

results.title: Decentralized Trajectory Optimization for Multi-Agent Ergodic Exploration
results.reason: Mechanical: 'ergodic' and 'trajectory' both appear. (Independent note: in fact this is robotic ergodic-coverage exploration, a different ergodic-theory application from ergodic-decomposition diagnosis, but rule forces hit.)

results.title: AgentDiagnose: An Open Toolkit for Diagnosing LLM Agent Trajectories
results.reason: Diagnostic toolkit for agent trajectories but uses heuristic annotation, not ergodic-theory decomposition.

results.title: TrajTune: Trajectory-Based Prompt Optimization for LLM Agents
results.reason: Prompt-optimization framework using trajectories as input; no ergodic-theoretic framing.

results.title: The Birkhoff Ergodic Theorem with Applications
results.reason: Mechanical: 4 content_words match in snippet.

results.title: A Note on the Birkhoff Ergodic Theorem
results.reason: Mechanical: 4 content_words match; pure-math note on the theorem itself.

results.title: Ergodic theory - Wikipedia
results.reason: Mechanical: 3 content_words match; encyclopedia overview of the foundational framework.

results.title: A Note on the Birkhoff Ergodic Theorem
results.reason: Mechanical: 3 content_words match in title+snippet.

results.title: Birkhoff Ergodic Theorem - an overview
results.reason: Mechanical: 'ergodic' and 'Birkhoff' both appear.

results.title: Model-Agnostic Solutions for Deep Reinforcement Learning in Non-Ergodic Contexts
results.reason: Skeptical judgment: only 'ergodic' (inside 'non-ergodic') matches; paper addresses non-ergodicity via Kelly-criterion path dependence on reward, not ergodic decomposition of (state,action) trajectories of LLM agents into invariant components -- the mechanism and object of study differ enough that this is adjacent, not functionally equivalent.

results.title: Deep neural networks from the perspective of ergodic theory
results.reason: Mechanical: 'ergodic' + 'invariant measure' (in 'invariant measures'). Also substantively the closest prior-art: applies ergodic-theory invariant-measure view to NN parameter distribution via SGD-as-Markov-process.

results.title: Non-ergodicity in reinforcement learning: robustness via ergodic transformations
results.reason: Skeptical judgment: only 'ergodic' matches; this is the reward-transformation-for-RL-robustness line, which uses ergodicity vocabulary but does not perform an ergodic decomposition of trajectories nor diagnose policy-lock-in via invariant components -- not functionally equivalent prior art.

results.title: Reinforcement learning with non-ergodic reward increments: robustness via ergodicity transformations
results.reason: Mechanical: 'ergodic' (and inside 'ergodicity') plus 'trajectory' (inside 'trajectories') both appear.

results.title: Description of the Training Process of Neural Networks via Ergodic Theorem: Ghost nodes
results.reason: Skeptical judgment: only 'ergodic' matches; describes NN training (parameter dynamics under SGD), not long-horizon agent trajectory ergodic decomposition or capability-mode diagnosis. Adjacent but not the same mechanism or problem space.

results.title: An ergodicity perspective on reinforcement learning
results.reason: Mechanical: 'ergodic' (inside 'ergodicity') and 'time average' both appear.

results.title: Ergodic Theory and Agentic Ideation in AI
results.reason: Skeptical judgment: only 'ergodic' matches; informal Medium post about agentic ideation does not constitute genuine prior art -- it neither formalizes ergodic decomposition of trajectories nor publishes any test statistic or empirical result. Title-level coincidence only.

results.title: Fast Ergodic Search With Kernel Functions
results.reason: Robotic ergodic-coverage search; distinct application of 'ergodic' (information-theoretic coverage) from ergodic-decomposition diagnosis.

results.title: IterResearch: Rethinking Long-Horizon Agents with Interaction Scaling
results.reason: Long-horizon agent benchmark paper; zero content-word overlap and no ergodic-theory mechanism.

results.title: Plan-and-Act: Improving Planning of Agents for Long-Horizon Tasks
results.reason: Planning framework, no overlap, no ergodic theory.

results.title: UltraHorizon: Benchmarking Agent Capabilities in Ultra Long-Horizon Scenarios
results.reason: Benchmark for long-horizon agents with 200k-token trajectories; aggregate snapshot evaluation, no ergodic decomposition.

results.title: AMA-Bench: Evaluating Long-Horizon Memory for Agentic Applications
results.reason: Memory benchmark; no content-word overlap and no ergodic-theory framing.

results.title: The Long-Horizon Task Mirage? Diagnosing Where and Why Agentic Systems Break
results.reason: Diagnostic study of long-horizon failure modes; no overlap, no ergodic content.

disagreement_details.implication: primary may have been over-strict (toward hit): primary chose hit=true on a non-forced (overlap=1) result. Fresh judgment finds the reward-transformation/Kelly-criterion mechanism is adjacent but not functionally equivalent to ergodic-decomposition of agent trajectories. Primary appears to have been generous to non-forced matches, possibly inflating the hit count.
primary may have been over-strict (toward hit): same non-ergodic-reward-transformation line as above; overlap=1 should leave room for skepticism. Fresh agent rates this as adjacent, not prior art.

primary may have been over-strict (toward hit): primary marked a NN-training-dynamics paper as hit on overlap=1. Fresh judgment: training-time parameter ergodicity is a different object than (state,action) trajectory ergodic components.

primary may have been over-strict (toward hit): a Medium blog post with only the word 'ergodic' in common and no formal mechanism does not constitute genuine prior art. Primary may have over-weighted title-level coincidence with informal community discourse.
